title: OpenAI plans a $30bn Georgia data centre, and a charm offensive to match
excerpt: Building a data centre used to be a quiet affair. Not any more. OpenAI’s plan for a giant new site in Georgia arrives with a charm offensive attached, because the industry has learned that the hard part is no longer the concrete. It is the neighbours. The company will spend tens of billions of dollars […]
content_html:
Building a data centre used to be a quiet affair. Not any more. OpenAI’s plan for a giant new site in Georgia arrives with a charm offensive attached, because the industry has learned that the hard part is no longer the concrete. It is the neighbours.

The company will spend tens of billions of dollars on a complex near Savannah, Bloomberg reported. It has contracted with Georgia Power for 3.2 gigawatts of electricity in Effingham County. A single gigawatt is enough to run about 750,000 homes.

Codenamed Project Camellia, the site would rank among OpenAI’s largest, DataCenterDynamics reported.

The figures are staggered. OpenAI has committed $20bn in the county to qualify for an incentive package due for a vote this week. At the full 3.2GW, the bill would top $30bn, said Sachin Katti, its vice-president of compute strategy.

Several hundred megawatts come online from 2028, the rest by 2032. The company is hunting for partners to help pay for and build it.

The more interesting shift is who now holds the pen. OpenAI is no longer just renting these places. It is designing them, and it wants to give the designs away.

Open-sourcing the factory

Stargate, OpenAI’s infrastructure programme, began as a joint venture with Oracle and SoftBank. Now the partners vary, and OpenAI is leading the planning itself.

“There’s a bunch of lessons around how these things should be designed,” Katti said, that “cut costs as well as make it faster to build.” The company plans to open-source its blueprints for data centres, hardware, and networking. It is echoing the Open Compute Project that Meta started in 2011.

A selective doubling-down

This is not blanket expansion. OpenAI has recently paused projects in the UK and Norway, and declined to grow its flagship Texas campus, trimming spending as it eyes a stock-market debut. Rivals keep pushing. Anthropic has leased more than 300MW from SpaceX’s Colossus 1 in Memphis, and Meta has committed over $250bn to a single Louisiana campus.

Winning over Effingham County

The pitch to locals is the tell. Data centres now draw fierce local opposition over power prices and water.

So OpenAI says it will pay the full cost of the electrical work, which state rules bar it from passing to existing customers, and it will throttle power at peak times. It promises a closed-loop cooling system, hundreds of millions in taxes, a community fund, and AI credits for local students.

It will also listen. The company met county officials and starts talking to residents on Thursday, said global affairs chief Chris Lehane, and it will commission an independent audit of its promises. “All issues are going to be local,” he said.

Whether that is enough to keep a 3.2-gigawatt machine welcome in a Georgia county is the question the next few years will answer.

sentences.zh: 竞争对手不断逼近。

sentences.zh: Anthropic 已从位于孟菲斯的 SpaceX Colossus 1 租赁了超过 300 兆瓦的电力，而 Meta 已向路易斯安那州的一个园区投入超过 2500 亿美元。

sentences.zh: 赢得埃芬汉县

sentences.zh: 因此，OpenAI 表示，它将支付电力工程的全部费用，该州的规定禁止将其转嫁给现有客户，并且它将在高峰时段限制电力。

sentences.en: It promises a closed-loop cooling system, hundreds of millions in taxes, a community fund, and AI credits for local students.
sentences.zh: 它承诺提供闭环冷却系统、数亿美元的税收、社区基金以及为当地学生提供的人工智能学分。

sentences.zh: 它也会听。

sentences.en: The company met county officials and starts talking to residents on Thursday, said global affairs chief Chris Lehane, and it will commission an independent audit of its promises.
sentences.zh: 全球事务主管克里斯·勒哈恩 (Chris Lehane) 表示，该公司周四会见了县官员，并开始与居民交谈，并将委托对其承诺进行独立审计。

sentences.en: “All issues are going to be local,” he said.
sentences.zh: “所有问题都将是本地问题，”他说。

blocks.html_en: Whether that is enough to keep a 3.2-gigawatt machine welcome in a Georgia county is the question the next few years will answer.

sentences.en: Whether that is enough to keep a 3.2-gigawatt machine welcome in a Georgia county is the question the next few years will answer.
sentences.zh: 这是否足以让 3.2 吉瓦的机器在佐治亚州的一个县受到欢迎，这是未来几年将回答的问题。

content_html_zh:
建设数据中心曾经是一件安静的事情。 不再。 OpenAI 在佐治亚州建立一个巨型新站点的计划伴随着魅力攻势，因为业界已经认识到困难的部分不再是混凝土。 这是邻居们。

据彭博社报道，该公司将斥资数百亿美元在萨凡纳附近建造一座综合大楼。 它已与乔治亚电力公司签订了埃芬汉县 3.2 吉瓦电力合同。 1 吉瓦足以为大约 750,000 个家庭供电。

据 DataCenterDynamics 报道，该网站代号为 Project Camellia，将跻身 OpenAI 最大网站之列。

数字是交错的。 OpenAI 已承诺在该县投资 200 亿美元，以获得本周投票的激励方案。 该公司负责计算战略的副总裁 Sachin Katti 表示，按 3.2GW 计算，该费用将超过 300 亿美元。

从 2028 年起，将有数百兆瓦发电，其余的将在 2032 年发电。 该公司正在寻找合作伙伴来帮助支付和建设它。

更有趣的转变是谁现在握笔。 OpenAI 不再只是租用这些地方。 它正在设计它们，并且想要将设计赠送出去。

工厂开源

Stargate 是 OpenAI 的基础设施项目，最初是与 Oracle 和 SoftBank 的合资企业。 现在合作伙伴各不相同，OpenAI 正在主导规划。

卡蒂说：“关于如何设计这些东西，有很多经验教训，可以降低成本并加快构建速度。” 该公司计划开源其数据中心、硬件和网络的蓝图。 它呼应了 Meta 于 2011 年启动的开放计算项目。

选择性加倍

这不是全面扩张。 OpenAI 最近暂停了在英国和挪威的项目，并拒绝扩大其德克萨斯州旗舰园区的规模，并因着眼于股票上市而削减支出。 竞争对手不断逼近。 Anthropic 已从位于孟菲斯的 SpaceX Colossus 1 租赁了超过 300 兆瓦的电力，而 Meta 已向路易斯安那州的一个园区投入超过 2500 亿美元。

赢得埃芬汉县

对当地人的宣传就是答案。 数据中心现在因电价和水价而引起当地的强烈反对。

因此，OpenAI 表示，它将支付电力工程的全部费用，该州的规定禁止将其转嫁给现有客户，并且它将在高峰时段限制电力。 它承诺提供闭环冷却系统、数亿美元的税收、社区基金以及为当地学生提供的人工智能学分。

它也会听。 全球事务主管克里斯·勒哈恩 (Chris Lehane) 表示，该公司周四会见了县官员，并开始与居民交谈，并将委托对其承诺进行独立审计。 “所有问题都将是本地问题，”他说。

这是否足以让 3.2 吉瓦的机器在佐治亚州的一个县受到欢迎，这是未来几年将回答的问题。
